tags: Authentication required for `git push` in the same terminal after login with `ncryptsec`
Git issue: Authentication required for `git push` in the same terminal after login with `ncryptsec`
content:
On ubuntu 24 lts.

First I ran `ngit account logout`

Then I ran `ngit account login --interactive` and followed the promts to login with `ncryptsec`.

then I ran `git config --list --show-origin` and got this :
```
...
file:/home/******/.gitconfig    nostr.nsec=ncryptsec1qgg2mwk3lgn5...
file:/home/***/.gitconfig    nostr.npub=npub1manln...
...
```

Then I ran  `git push` I got this:
```
Error: Authentication required. Run 'ngit account login' first, then try again.

Caused by:
    failed to login without prompts a nsec is encrypted with a password
error: failed to push some refs to 'nostr://...'
```
All this in the same terminal, within the same hour, one right after the other.
ngit with ncryptsec doesn't seem to work. Am I missing something?

tags: Update install.sh pointing to the latest release
Git issue: Update install.sh pointing to the latest release
content:
Currently https://ngit.dev/install.sh points to `v2.4.4` while the latest release tag is `v2.5.0` at the moment of this writing.

In my automated update scripts I use this snippet to fetch / checkout the latest tag from a repo:

```
git checkout $(git describe --tags $(git rev-list --tags --max-count=1))
```

So `git describe --tags $(git rev-list --tags --max-count=1)` might help replace the static tag variable in the install shell script.

tags: fix: handle missing optional patch tags
git repository issue: fix: handle missing optional patch tags
content:
The 'author' can be extracted from the `From: ` and `Date: ` lines.

The 'committer' date can be extracted the `From ` mbox line, falling back to 'author' value.

the commit message should be extracted also, initially as a fallback for the `description` tag as before we had issues reliably extracting it. nostr:nprofile1qqsqqqqqq9g9uljgjfcyd6dm4fegk8em2yfz0c3qp3tc6mntkrrhawgffc29f has done some work on this.

For the `parent-commit` we need a stable 'best guess': the commit with the largest 'committer' date which which is less the patches 'committer' date. 

Here is an test case which i guess was generated with n34:

nostr:nevent1qvzqqqqx2ypzqqqqqqq4qhn7fzf8q3hfhw489zcl8dg3yflzyqx90rtwdwcvwl4eqqs2xzpsxjd3ujdprp0kcxtgfg0ush9nu3wykmj909xuejktv8alecsqct0qg#bug #compatibility

This should enable these patches to appear as `pr/` branches, and not just appliable and downloadable via `ngit list` which is the current behaviour.

tags: Give option to list stuff assigned to me
content: As a logged in user I would like to be able to use `ngit list` to list patches assigned to me.

content:

#bug

tags: git repository issue: `database.query` not returning specific coordinate
content:
`database.query` not returning specific coordinate

nostr:naddr1qqrkxcf3xyuxzcgzyzsq3hh327t0h2dq6matqn5064cgj2zanl2stkj6s0lg4t2h5dty6qcyqqq80xgx4xm2l
when using filter:
```rust
    nostr::Filter::default()
        .kind(Kind::GitRepoAnnouncement)
        .identifiers(
            repo_coordinates
                .iter()
                .map(|c| c.identifier.clone())
                .collect::<Vec<String>>(),
        )
        .authors(
            repo_coordinates
                .iter()
                .map(|c| c.public_key)
                .collect::<Vec<PublicKey>>(),
        )
```
it returns the event when querying by the event id.
`7f7a9fac2584002b6beb97d62989affdbd4b13659b277fa9d90a034f44dbdee2`
other coordinates seems to work ok eg nostr:naddr1qqzxuemfwsqs6amnwvaz7tmwdaejumr0dspzpgqgmmc409hm4xsdd74sf68a2uyf9pwel4g9mfdg8l5244t6x4jdqvzqqqrhnym0k2qj
